title: symplocos cochinchinensis subsp. thwaitesii
intro: Symplocos cochinchinensis subsp. thwaitesii, also known as the Symplocos or the Indian alder, is a plant species in the Symplocaceae family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating respiratory issues, including bronchitis and coughs. The herbal preparations of Symplocos cochinchinensis subsp. thwaitesii include decoctions, infusions, and extracts, which can be used to treat various ailments and promote overall well-being.
